data.body: RESOLVED BY DESIGN — not needed. Joe's adjudication of the gate's T12 finding: the gate is CONFIG-GATED and fail-open when off, so disabling it via config IS the kill-switch/rollback — turning the config off reverts claim to current behavior instantly. No separate rollback mechanism is needed. The global enable/disable flag is inherent to the gate's config surface; recorded in the epic 5fd2 config Success Criterion. This finding was an advisory (not block) over-call the Pass-2 verifier's 'no-existing-mitigation' check should have softened (config-gating IS the mitigation) — good three-pass calibration data: advisory finding -> human adjudication -> dismissed. Closing won't-do/superseded.
